human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1602 scope, endpoint-security specialty boundary, coordination-handoffs-and-routing workflow coverage, cross-industry source-class coverage, functionality mapping, research summary, refusal triggers, specification-based runtime posture, and validated evidence consistency.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Evaluation coverage exceeds the issue minimum of 12 scenarios and covers output fidelity, refusals, coordination, handoffs, blocker escalation, closure, low-confidence handling, conflicting sources, and tenant adaptation.

Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to endpoint-security advisory work, endpoint evidence analysis, remediation planning, handoff packaging, blocker management, and closure confirmation.

Authority sources cover NIST CSF, NIST endpoint malware, patch, control, and monitoring guidance; CISA incident/vulnerability playbooks, KEV, BOD 22-01, and StopRansomware; CIS Controls; Microsoft Defender and Intune; Apple Platform Deployment; and FTC regulated caveats.

Scenarios cover realistic endpoint records and output shapes, including prioritized remediation plans, control mappings, rollout and rollback sequencing, explicit refusals, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separate frequent-use trusted-build promotion records implementation review, regression tests, monitoring, rollback, and tenant-isolation evidence.
